\paragraph{Audit: $M(u)=M_0+uM_1$ and the two-step operator $M_0M_1+M_1M_0$ (real-input-40, output potential)}
We split the 40-state adjacency by the output bit $e\in\{0,1\}$ as $M(u)=M_0+uM_1$.
On this kernel, $M_1^2$ is \emph{not} zero, even restricted to the largest SCC of the underlying directed graph.

\begin{equation*}
\boxed{\ \rho(M_0M_1+M_1M_0)=y_{\max},\quad y_{\max}^4-6y_{\max}^3+9y_{\max}^2-y_{\max}-1=0\ }
\end{equation*}
\noindent Numerical certificate (double precision):
\begin{equation*}
\rho(M_0M_1+M_1M_0)\approx 3.5961546760086334,\qquad y_\max\approx 3.5961546760086334.
\end{equation*}

\noindent Explicit 2-step witness for $M_1^2\ne 0$ (two consecutive $e=1$ steps):
\begin{align*}
&v_0=\text{(101;p_x=0,p_y=0)}\ (\# 20)\xrightarrow{(x,y)=(0,0),\ d=0,\ e=1}v_1=\text{(010;p_x=0,p_y=0)}\ (\# 12)\\
&v_1=\text{(010;p_x=0,p_y=0)}\ (\# 12)\xrightarrow{(x,y)=(1,1),\ d=2,\ e=1}v_2=\text{(0-12;p_x=1,p_y=1)}\ (\# 27) .
\end{align*}
